museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.curatorNote: Initial researched draft for July 2024. Recent enough to need careful human review before verification.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: Five source-noted moments or context markers.

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero launches.
items.body: A source-noted month marker; regional timing is kept visible where it matters.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: Dawntrail keeps Final Fantasy XIV moving.
items.body: A period-context note for the month, written cautiously where the evidence is broader than one exact day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: Dungeons of Hinterberg and Kunitsu-Gami give the month distinctive colour.
items.body: A period-context note for the month, written cautiously where the evidence is broader than one exact day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: Layoffs and studio closures give the year's successes a darker industry frame.
items.body: A year-level context marker included to frame the month without claiming a new event happened on a specific day.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

items.title: Nintendo acknowledges a Switch successor announcement is due within the fiscal year.
items.body: A source-noted 2024 context marker; the console itself was not fully revealed during these exhibits.
items.sourceNote: See source notes for this exhibit; exact dates should be rechecked during human edit.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, with month-specific anchors first where possible.

items.title: Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown
items.body: Ubisoft gives an old name a sharp side-scrolling form.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2024 calendar lists January 18.

items.title: Palworld
items.body: A survival-crafting monster game becomes an enormous online argument.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2024 calendar lists January 19.

items.title: Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth
items.body: Ryu Ga Gotoku turns a massive RPG into a holiday suitcase.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2024 calendar lists January 26.

items.title: Helldivers 2
items.body: Arrowhead accidentally makes one of the year's defining co-op rituals.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot/Helldivers sources list February 8.

items.title: Balatro
items.body: A poker roguelike becomes the year's most dangerous little machine.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2024 calendar lists February 20.

items.title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
items.body: Square's remake trilogy opens into a road trip of memory and spectacle.
items.sourceNote: FFVII Rebirth sources list February 29.

items.title: Dragon's Dogma 2
items.body: Capcom returns with emergent fantasy friction and pawn chatter.
items.sourceNote: Dragon's Dogma 2 sources list March 22.

items.title: Animal Well
items.body: A dense puzzle-box platformer becomes a spoiler-sensitive online object.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2024 calendar lists May 9.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PS5 Pro is announced and launched
items.body: Sony sells a premium mid-generation machine into a market newly sensitive to price.
items.sourceNote: PlayStation Blog lists September 10 announcement and November 7 launch.

items.title: Nintendo acknowledges a Switch successor
items.body: The next Nintendo machine becomes official enough to shape 2024 without being shown.
items.sourceNote: Gematsu reports Furukawa's May 2024 statement.

items.title: Xbox loosens the exclusivity map
items.body: Microsoft confirms selected Xbox games will go to rival consoles.
items.sourceNote: AP reports the February 2024 Xbox business update.

items.title: Industry layoffs become part of the archive
items.body: The year's excellent games sit beside studio closures, cuts and a sense of instability.
items.sourceNote: Industry-layoff context should be source-checked during human edit.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How players discovered, argued about and shared the month.

items.title: Co-op becomes propaganda comedy
items.body: Helldivers 2 turns community goals and satire into daily online performance.

items.title: Small games become huge rooms
items.body: Balatro and Animal Well show how mystery, systems and streamer discovery can carry a year.

items.title: Hardware discourse gets expensive
items.body: PS5 Pro turns frame rates, upscaling and price into autumn's recurring argument.

items.title: Layoffs sit under the celebration
items.body: Every showcase and awards night is shadowed by workers and studios disappearing.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A short atmospheric reading from the player side of recent history.

items.title: Joy and exhaustion shared the room
items.body: Astro Bot and Balatro sparkled while layoffs made the industry feel bruised.

items.title: Premium hardware felt harder to justify
items.body: PS5 Pro made fidelity feel desirable and financially awkward.

items.title: The past kept returning
items.body: Rebirth, Silent Hill 2, Paper Mario and Indiana Jones all made memory feel current.

items.title: Indies felt like the bloodstream
items.body: Balatro, Animal Well and UFO 50 showed that small teams could define the year's mood.
